summary: Implemented dirname caching using a Map. The cache reduces path parsing overhead during recursive directory creation. Benchmark shows ~20.8% improvement (5237.11 -> 6326.47 ops/sec).

Replaced Object.assign wrapper with direct property assignments to eliminate object creation overhead. However, benchmark shows no improvement (6309.04 vs baseline 6326.47 ops/sec, -0.27%). The Object.assign overhead at module initialization time does not impact runtime performance in this hot path.

Inlined pathArg validation directly into mkdirp and mkdirpSync entry points, eliminating function call overhead. The null byte check and Windows character validation are now performed inline, enabling better JIT optimization. Benchmark shows ~6.9% improvement (6326.47 -> 6764.95 ops/sec).

Implemented cached Promise wrapper functions for mkdirAsync and statAsync to avoid repeated closure creation in the default fs case. However, benchmark shows regression (6166.67 vs baseline 6764.95 ops/sec, -8.8%). The overhead of additional conditionals and function indirection outweighs any benefits from caching the Promise wrappers. V8's JIT likely already optimizes closure creation efficiently, making this optimization counterproductive.
